data.title: Bahrain Central Bank Launches Licensing and Regulatory Framework for Stablecoin Issuers
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - The Central Bank of Bahrain announced the launch of a licensing and regulatory framework for stablecoin issuers, aimed at ensuring the safe integration of stablecoins into the financial system. The new regulations allow licensed institutions to issue single-currency stablecoins backed by the Bahraini dinar, the U.S. dollar or other approved fiat currencies. The move aims to reduce the risks of unregulated stablecoins, enhance investor confidence and promote sustainable growth in the industry. (Bahrain News Agency)

data.title: Solana bot scam on GitHub steals crypto from users
data.description:
A fake GitHub repository posing as a Solana trading bot was used to distribute obscured malware that stole crypto wallet credentials, according to cybersecurity firm SlowMist.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/solana-trading-bot-github-malware-scam?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3F_nocache%3D1751638539790%26cache%3D1751638539790&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Crypto Market Sees $35.47 Million in Liquidations Over Four Hours
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market experienced liquidations totaling $35.47 million in the past four hours. Of this amount, short positions accounted for $6.65 million, while long positions saw liquidations amounting to $28.82 million.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs See Significant Inflows
data.description: According to TechFlow, on July 4, data from the on-chain analysis platform Lookonchain revealed that ten U.S. Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) experienced a net inflow of 2,617 BTC. Additionally, nine Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 36,439 ETH. These figures indicate a notable increase in investment activity within these cryptocurrency funds.

data.title: The “Insider Whale” Bitcoin short position has turned from loss to profit, with a current floating profit of $50,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to @ai_9684xtpa’s monitoring, BTC may be affected by the BTC fossil hand. BTC fell below $108,000 in a short period of time. With the continuous increase in positions in the past half hour, the BTC short positions of "insider whales" have turned losses into profits - currently holding short positions of 45.47 million US dollars, with an opening price of 107,806.6 US dollars and a floating profit of 50,000 US dollars.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin futures trading volume fell 20% in June, the market may be at risk of falling
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: In the first five months of 2025, the average monthly trading volume of Bitcoin futures was $1.93 trillion, but the total trading volume of Bitcoin futures on centralized exchanges in June was only about $1.55 trillion, which means that the trading volume in June was about 20% lower than the monthly average trading volume from the beginning of the year to date.
A similar situation occurred last year, with the total trading volume of Bitcoin futures in June 2024 falling 15.7% month-on-month. Although trading volume rebounded the following month, the average monthly trading volume of Bitcoin futures from June to September 2024 was still low, at only $1.53 trillion. If this month's data is a reference, the crypto market may be about to usher in another quiet summer. (The Block)

data.title: Federal Reserve Expected to Ease Policy in September Amid Data Influences
data.description: According to Odaily, Nationwide Financial's Chief Economist Kathy Bostjancic has indicated that a significant amount of data in the coming months will impact the Federal Reserve's decisions. If current trends persist, the Federal Reserve is expected to implement easing measures in September. Bostjancic predicts that the Fed will reduce interest rates by 25 basis points in September, October, and December. However, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell may adopt a cautious approach by supporting a rate cut in September while emphasizing that it does not signal further easing. If inflation worsens, the Fed may pause its actions.

data.title: The sixth address of an ancient giant whale holding 10,000 BTC has been activated after being dormant for about 14 years
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Whale Alert monitoring, another Bitcoin address of an ancient giant whale that has been dormant for 14.2 years has just been activated. The address contains 10,000 Bitcoins (1,080,168,087 US dollars). It is reported that this is the sixth Bitcoin address that has been dormant for more than 14 years to be activated today.

data.title: Economist: The Fed may cut interest rates by 25 basis points in September, October and December
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Kathy Bostjancic, chief economist of Nationwide Financial, said that a lot of data will affect the Fed's decision-making in the next two months. If the current trend continues, the Fed should be able to implement loose policies in September. He believes that the Fed will cut interest rates by 25 basis points in September, October and December respectively, but Powell may balance all parties by "not over-committing": that is, supporting the September rate cut and emphasizing that this is not a signal of further easing, and suspending action if inflation worsens. (Jinshi)

data.title: E-sports company Ninjas in Pyjamas enters Bitcoin mining, expects to produce 60 Bitcoins per month
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: NIP Group, the parent company of e-sports company Ninjas in Pyjamas, announced its entry into the field of Bitcoin mining and has acquired an unknown number of Bitcoin mining equipment. The company estimates that its mining operations will produce 60 Bitcoins per month, or $6.5 million. (Decrypt)

data.title: Warning Traders: $3.6 Billion in Options Set to Expire
data.description:

Bitcoin’s put-to-call ratio slightly favors bearish bets at 1. 05, while Ethereum shows a more cautious stance with a 1. 25 ratio. Both cryptocurrencies are trading above their maximum pain prices, hinting at possible price. . .
